metric_value: HP avowed strategy: lead market in consumer IT including access PCs, imaging, and new CE products
notes: Aberdeen frames this against the product failure to contrast aspiration vs. execution

metric_value: Consistently high quality photographs over 6 months of use
notes: Positive finding; photo transfer process also straightforward once working

metric_name: ACD See Missing DLL Under Windows ME
metric_value: ACD See image display software had a missing .dll; unusable as primary image-handler
notes: First installation hitch in May 2001; resolved by using alternate software

metric_name: HP Photo Transfer Software XP Incompatibility
metric_value: HP bundled photo transfer software interferes with Windows XP native camera support; causes photo transfer failure
notes: Root cause: software conflict; undocumented by HP; support technician unaware

metric_name: In-Place Upgrade vs. Clean Install Caveat
metric_value: HP web site claims XP native support but only works with clean install, not in-place upgrade
notes: Technically correct but misleading; most consumers follow Microsoft's recommended in-place upgrade

metric_name: Support Call Cost vs. Camera Retail Price
metric_value: A single tech support call costs HP more than the retail price of the camera
notes: Based on Aberdeen's own help-desk cost research conducted over several years

metric_value: 45 minutes to resolve immediate problem; native XP support still not functioning afterward
notes: Technician gave high grades but problem incompletely resolved

metric_value: Workaround (do not install bundled HP photo transfer software) not documented on HP support site
notes: Failure to document creates wave of support calls from XP upgraders

metric_value: Dissatisfied consumers will go elsewhere for high-tech appliances; HP will lose consumer market share
notes: Aberdeen prediction; HP did eventually exit consumer digital camera market

metric_value: Fix internal process that facilitated lack-of-instructions fiasco
Keep instructions simple and predictable; consumer pain tolerance lower than IT professionals
Simplify the complex — easy to say, hard to do
notes: Third Aberdeen recommendation; terse but emphasizes core product design challenge

metric_value: Economic law of consumer electronics: cannot afford many support calls and still profit on mass-market products
notes: Aberdeen frames this as an inviolable constraint HP violated

metric_value: HP photography web site offers free photo publishing but is hard to find
notes: Positive feature undermined by poor navigation and discoverability

metric_name: Windows XP Compatibility Lesson for Consumers
metric_value: Programs and devices working with prior Windows may not work under XP; always check support site for XP-specific updates
notes: Consumer advisory lesson generalizing beyond HP to industry-wide XP upgrade challenges
